title: Automated Perifusion System for Modifying Cell Culture Conditions
author: Whitticar, Strahler, et al.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Plug USB cable into the computer and attach the network cable adapter labeled “CBL-PC-PUMP”. The network cable goes into first pump where it is labeled “computer” on the bottom side. This is now “Pump 00”. Connect the next network cable from slot labeled “pump network” on pump 00 to the next pump in the slot labeled “computer”. This is now “Pump 01”. Plug in power supplies to both pumps (Pumps should beep upon power being connected).Label and fill syringes to appropriate level with no bubbles. (Tap on lab bench to loosen bubbles then expel air from needle).Place syringes in apparatus placing head of plunger in vice and tighten (screw direction is opposite of standard).Pull up and spin tab over barrel of syringe to secure in place.Connect hosing to the end of each syringe coming together at a “Y” junction. A single hose should then go completely through the heating apparatus and into the tube connected to the well. The peristaltic pump outflow should also be assembled and set to match the flow rate that will be programed.Squeeze white tab in on the side of the syringe slide and move until the second fluid needed is seen at the “Y” junction. Repeat this with the remaining pump containing the fluid that will be used when loading the islets. This time the slide should be moved until the fluid is seen filling up the well enough to make a meniscus with the objective lens.Open “SyringePumpProV1” on computer. Make sure pump 00 and pump 01 are loaded in pump worksheet. If not, reassure network and power cables are fully inserted and restart the program.Click “PPL” tab on top of screen, PPL Creator, Standard.See How to Create Program below.After making protocol for pump 00, right click on pump 00, upload PPL file. Allow program to run through the protocol until you hear a beep. Click “OK” on the popup which sometimes comes up behind the current window. Then, right click on pump 00 and click “refresh values”.Repeat steps 8–10 for pump 01.Click ‘run all’ on right of screen and turn on the peristaltic pump for outflow. The system must be balanced so the outflow nail is high enough for the well not to dry out over long periods.To pause the program, click “Stop/Pause” once. To resume, click “Start” again. To restart the program, click “Stop/Pause” twice and make sure the “Amount Dispensed” column resets to 0.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Plug USB cable into the computer and attach the network cable adapter labeled “CBL-PC-PUMP”. The network cable goes into first pump where it is labeled “computer” on the bottom side. This is now “Pump 00”. Connect the next network cable from slot labeled “pump network” on pump 00 to the next pump in the slot labeled “computer”. This is now “Pump 01”. Plug in power supplies to both pumps (Pumps should beep upon power being connected).

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Label and fill syringes to appropriate level with no bubbles. (Tap on lab bench to loosen bubbles then expel air from needle).

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Place syringes in apparatus placing head of plunger in vice and tighten (screw direction is opposite of standard).

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Pull up and spin tab over barrel of syringe to secure in place.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Connect hosing to the end of each syringe coming together at a “Y” junction. A single hose should then go completely through the heating apparatus and into the tube connected to the well. The peristaltic pump outflow should also be assembled and set to match the flow rate that will be programed.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Squeeze white tab in on the side of the syringe slide and move until the second fluid needed is seen at the “Y” junction. Repeat this with the remaining pump containing the fluid that will be used when loading the islets. This time the slide should be moved until the fluid is seen filling up the well enough to make a meniscus with the objective lens.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Open “SyringePumpProV1” on computer. Make sure pump 00 and pump 01 are loaded in pump worksheet. If not, reassure network and power cables are fully inserted and restart the program.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Click “PPL” tab on top of screen, PPL Creator, Standard.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: See How to Create Program below.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: After making protocol for pump 00, right click on pump 00, upload PPL file. Allow program to run through the protocol until you hear a beep. Click “OK” on the popup which sometimes comes up behind the current window. Then, right click on pump 00 and click “refresh values”.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: Click ‘run all’ on right of screen and turn on the peristaltic pump for outflow. The system must be balanced so the outflow nail is high enough for the well not to dry out over long periods.

steps.title: Automated Perfusion Protocol
steps.instruction: To pause the program, click “Stop/Pause” once. To resume, click “Start” again. To restart the program, click “Stop/Pause” twice and make sure the “Amount Dispensed” column resets to 0.
